primary_source: Whittaker, 1903. On the partial differential equations of mathematical physics. Math. Annalen 57:333-355. DOI: 10.1007/BF01444290.
title: QA Whittaker Rational Direction S^1 FAIL [266] - W2 declared min(sin(separation)) too large
_fixture_purpose: FAIL fixture: declares declared_min_sin_separation = 0.01 for D_9. The actual minimum sin(angular separation) across all 1485 distinct pairs of D_9 is approximately 6.116e-3, which is less than the declared 0.01. The W2 theorem only guarantees the LOOSE bound sin(Delta theta) >= 1/G_max^2 ~ 7.3e-6 (for m=9), not any specific tighter value. The fixture's declared 0.01 is an OVERCLAIM; validator independently computes the true minimum via all-pairs cross-product and rejects with WRD_W2.
source_attribution: (Whittaker, 1903) On the partial differential equations of mathematical physics, Math. Annalen 57:333-355, DOI: 10.1007/BF01444290.

witnesses.name: min_sin_overclaim
witnesses._note: Validator must find a pair of D_9 directions with sin(separation) < 0.01 and reject.

fail_ledger.rationale: All-pairs scan finds (b1,e1)=(some pair), (b2,e2)=(other pair) where sin(angular separation) ~ 6.116e-3 < 0.01. Validator rejects the declared lower bound.
